next_step: Add <details> accordion per wrong_q in drill_results.html — image lazy-loads on expand

title: Proper packaging — macOS installer + Windows distro
description: macOS: sign + notarize the app so Gatekeeper doesn't block it (eliminates xattr workaround); package as a proper .dmg with drag-to-install. Windows: PyInstaller build on Windows produces .exe; wrap in NSIS or Inno Setup installer. Both need an update mechanism that replaces the binary without touching app.db and question_bank/.
next_step: macOS first: get Apple Developer ID ($99/yr) then codesign + notarytool; Windows: set up a Windows build VM or GitHub Actions runner for cross-compile
notes: Current workaround (xattr -cr) is fine for one laptop; becomes painful at scale

title: Fix MathML rendering artifacts in question PNGs
description: 8 AK images show raw 'msup' superscript tag in their text layer — source PDF issue (College Board embedded MathML text alongside rendered formula). PNG render is visually correct but text layer is polluted. Affects: 290cdc2c / cfff8f8e / 252a3b3a / dd8ac009 / ce579859 / d139cf4b / 82c8325f / fa2771d5
next_step: Verify AK images are still visually readable (likely yes). If not re-render at higher DPI. Handle alongside T04 manual answer editor since all 8 need a manual review anyway.
notes: All Math: Equivalent Expressions x1 / Nonlinear Functions x5 / Circles x2. Source PDFs confirmed to have MathML in text layer — not a pymupdf bug.

title: DB schema migration strategy
description: app.db persists through app updates so schema changes (new columns/tables) must be applied as incremental migrations — never by regenerating the DB
next_step: Add schema_version key to config table; init_db() checks version and applies numbered migration blocks sequentially; each migration ships as a standalone .py script too so it can be run manually on Khushi's laptop
notes: init_db() already handles new tables via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS; column additions need ALTER TABLE wrapped in try/except since SQLite doesn't support IF NOT EXISTS on ALTER

title: Skill mastery inference
description: Infer mastery band per skill from weighted accuracy (L1=1x / L2=2x / L3=3x), coverage %, and 14-day recency decay. Bands: not_started / exploring / developing / proficient / mastered / review_due. Show on dashboard skill table (per difficulty row) and as aggregated skill table on progress page.
next_step: Implemented: get_skill_mastery() + get_progress_stats() in db.py; mastery badges on home.html; redesigned progress page with stat cards + accuracy trend chart + mastery table
notes: 14-day recency threshold chosen for 3-month SAT sprint timeline
